checks.name: 1.richardson: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.jacobi: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.gs: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.sor: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.ssor: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.ic_T4: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.ic_T8: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.ic_T16: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.ic_T31: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.two_level: component loop == M^-1(Nx+b) == x + M^-1(b-Ax), 5 iters
1.rich==jac: Richardson(alpha_opt) has M = I/alpha = 2/h^2 I == D, i.e. alpha_opt = h^2/2 (the suite's Jacobi-no-op: constant diagonal)
1.ic-ldl: M0 == Lhat blkdiag(A_PP, diag(schur)) Lhat^T (innovations form, all four T)
2.jacobi: rho(T_J) == cos(pi h) == mu
2.gs: rho(T_GS) == cos^2(pi h) == mu^2
2.omega: 2/(1+sin pi h) == 2/(1+sqrt(1-mu^2)) (Trefethen-notes form)
2.sor: rho(T_SOR(omega_opt)) == omega_opt - 1 == (1-sin pi h)/(1+sin pi h) (defective pair at the kink: eig tol 1e-6)
2.sor-form: omega_opt - 1 == (1-sin pi h)/(1+sin pi h)
2.sor-curve: rho(T_SOR(omega)) eigvals == quadratic-root formula on 192-pt grid in (0,2)
2.sor-above: rho == omega - 1 for all grid omega >= omega_opt
2.sor-kink: argmin of the omega-curve == omega_opt (within 5e-5 fine grid)
2.slope.ic_T31: exact preconditioner, error dead after ONE sweep

2.slopes: measured asymptotic rate == rho for every method, rel err < 1% (richardson 1.12e-16, jacobi 1.12e-16, gs 4.48e-16, sor 2.27e-04, ssor 6.73e-15, ic_T4 2.23e-16, ic_T8 2.23e-16, ic_T16 1.24e-15, two_level 2.52e-16)
2.iters: every method reaches 1e-8 (caps not hit); Richardson == Jacobi iteration-for-iteration (3609 each)
2.gs-half: GS iterations ~ half of Jacobi (rho_GS = rho_J^2): 1870 vs 3609
3.whiten.jacobi: step-1 GD on C^-T A C^-1 mapped back by C^-1 == M-preconditioned Richardson, 60 iterates
3.whiten.ssor: step-1 GD on C^-T A C^-1 mapped back by C^-1 == M-preconditioned Richardson, 60 iterates
3.whiten.ic_T4: step-1 GD on C^-T A C^-1 mapped back by C^-1 == M-preconditioned Richardson, 60 iterates
3.whiten.ic_T8: step-1 GD on C^-T A C^-1 mapped back by C^-1 == M-preconditioned Richardson, 60 iterates
3.whiten.ic_T16: step-1 GD on C^-T A C^-1 mapped back by C^-1 == M-preconditioned Richardson, 60 iterates
3.whiten.ic_T31: step-1 GD on C^-T A C^-1 mapped back by C^-1 == M-preconditioned Richardson, 60 iterates
3.whiten.two_level: step-1 GD on C^-T A C^-1 mapped back by C^-1 == M-preconditioned Richardson, 60 iterates
3.jacobi-coords: C_Jacobi = sqrt(2)/h I is a GLOBAL rescale (constant diagonal, unit conditional variances) -- the suite's Jacobi-no-op
3.ssor-coords: closed-form sweep-whitener K = sqrt(w/(2-w)) D^-1/2 (D/w + U) has K^T K == M_SSOR and K^T == chol(M_SSOR) (uniqueness)
3.nonsym: M_GS and M_SOR are NONSYMMETRIC (max |M - M^T| = 1/h^2 = 1089): no whitening reading exists for the sweeps
3.gs-cd: each GS micro-step == exact coordinate minimization of J (x_i <- x_i + r_i/A_ii)
3.gs-grad: dJ/dx_i == 0 immediately after each micro-step (Gibbs without noise, report 09 SS3)
3.gs-sweep: the full micro-step sequence == one GS sweep
3.sor-cd: each SOR micro-step == relax omega times PAST the coordinate minimum (x_i <- x_i + omega r_i/A_ii)
4.bridge: Sigma = A^-1 == h * x_min (1 - x_max) -- the Brownian-bridge covariance, scaled by h
4.Sigma-full: T=n run reproduces K == R^T R exactly
4.Sigma-tri: R[:, pivots] is upper triangular
4.Sigma-chol: P K P^T == Rp^T Rp and Rp == chol(P K P^T) (pivoted chol up to permutation)
4.Sigma-schur: d array after every step == diag of the Schur complement (unexplained variance bookkeeping)
4.A-full: T=n run reproduces K == R^T R exactly
4.A-tri: R[:, pivots] is upper triangular
4.A-chol: P K P^T == Rp^T Rp and Rp == chol(P K P^T) (pivoted chol up to permutation)
4.A-schur: d array after every step == diag of the Schur complement (unexplained variance bookkeeping)
4.pivots-Sigma: bridge-covariance pivot sequence starts at the midpoint and bisects: [17, 8, 25, 12, 21, 4, 29] (recorded in full in the JSON)
4.pivots-A: on A the pivots sweep the ODD nodes first (red-black order discovered by greedy variance): [1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15]...
4.duality: unpivoted Gram-Schmidt on B's columns gives R == chol(A)^T (09 SS4.3 / Shawe-Taylor SS5.2)
4.ic0: IC(0) == exact chol(A) on the chain (no fill-in on a path; report 15's truncated regression is exact here)
4.partial: first T=8 Gram-Schmidt rows == first 8 rows of chol(A)^T (partial GS in edge space == partial Cholesky on A)
4.pivoted-duality: PIVOTED Gram-Schmidt on B (pivot on residual column norm^2) == Alg 5.12 on A: same 16 pivots, same R rows
4.ic-kappa: factor-analysis surrogate kappa(M^-1 A) falls monotonically with T: 416 > 307 > 110 > 1; T=31 gives M == A exactly (kappa 1, one step)
4.ic-T31: rank-31 pivoted factor + 1x1 Schur residual reconstructs A EXACTLY (full rank on the chain -> one-step convergence)
5.gram: A == B^T B with the grounded (n+1) x n difference matrix (entries +-1/h, walls as grounded rails)
5.jacobi-volts: Jacobi update == neighbor-voltage average + h^2/2 source lift == D^-1(b - (L+U)x)
5.flux: y = B x* satisfies B^T y == b (edge currents of the true solution)
5.gd-ls: plain GD on 1/2||Bx - y||^2 (grad = B^T(Bx-y) = Ax - b) == Richardson on the normal equations, 60 iterates
5.diag-newton: Jacobi == diagonal-Newton on the least-squares objective (Hessian diag(B^T B) = 2/h^2 = D), 20 iterates
6.spectrum: additive Jacobi + 8 block averages: mu in [0.1565, 2.4411], kappa_eff = 15.603 (vs kappa = 440.7 plain: 28x), iterations 133 vs 3609 Jacobi
6.deflation: coarse space vs bottom-8 sine modes: largest principal angle 38.29 deg < 45 (eigenmodes_explainer convention); mode-1 overlap 0.989

6.projector: the coarse correction Z Ac^-1 Z^T A is the A-ORTHOGONAL projection onto range(Z): Pi^2 == Pi, A Pi == Pi^T A, Pi Z == Z (coarse residuals solved exactly in one shot -- the deflation reading)

6.energy: A-energy overlap of the coarse projection with mode 1 is only 0.296 (Euclidean overlap 0.989): piecewise-constant coarse functions are energy-rough; the win is spectral (kappa 441 -> 15.6), not modal
n8: A8 == B8^T B8 (entries 162/-81, +-9) and GS-on-B8 == chol(A8)^T

rhs.convention: green-tents unit heat, +1/h at node 8, -1/h at node 24

electrical.y_construction: y = B x* (edge currents of the true solution)
